zone_6b_notes: Grown as annual herb (bronze fennel for foliage/seeds) or as bulbing vegetable (Florence fennel) in Farmington. Direct sow after last frost or transplant carefully â€” fennel resents root disturbance. Florence fennel requires a long cool season; best as fall crop (sow July for October harvest) since Missouri heat causes premature bolting in spring. Bronze fennel is perennial-tough but not reliably hardy past Zone 6b. Important: plant fennel away from other vegetables â€” it releases allelopathic compounds that suppress germination and growth. Excellent for swallowtail butterflies.
type: perennial herb (tender in 6b); Florence fennel grown as annual

planting.best_planting_time: Direct sow May (herb use) or July (Florence bulb harvest in fall)
